key_insight: A combined DFT and machine-learning workflow identifies which five-metal high-entropy borides are most likely to form single-phase, mechanically stable AlB2-type compounds.
engineering_relevance: This provides a practical screening roadmap for designing ultra-hard borides with improved synthesizability before costly experiments.
method: Enumerated all 126 five-metal combinations from nine group 4–6 transition metals
Used entropy forming ability (EFA) from first-principles calculations to assess single-phase synthesizability
Computed mechanical properties with special quasi-random structures and trained ML models on the results
evidence_signal: The EFA predictions reportedly agree well with available experimental data, and compounds with Cr are often flagged as both mechanically unstable and less synthesizable.
watch_item: The conclusions are limited to the AlB2 structure and the sampled composition space, so predictions may not transfer to other crystal structures or off-grid chemistries without validation.
paper_title: Synthesizability and Mechanical Properties of High-Entropy Borides: First-Principles and Machine Learning Studies
